# domain-monitor

## Install

```bash
composer require rasuvaeff/domain-monitor
```

## Rules

- HTTP services require a PSR-18 client and a PSR-17 request factory.
- Host-only inputs go to host services; HTTP services require `http://` or `https://` URLs.
- `SslCertificateService` reads peer certificates best-effort and does not verify trust.
- `DomainMonitor` orchestrator runs all configured services in one call, reuses the HTTP response for probe + security headers + content check. Services are optional: `null` = check disabled.
- `DomainMonitor` catches exceptions per check and omits failed checks from the report (logs via PSR-3).

## API reference

### Run all checks at once (DomainMonitor orchestrator)

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitor;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitorOptions;

$monitor = new DomainMonitor(
    httpProbe: $probeService,
    ssl: $sslService,
    whois: $whoisService,
    dns: $dnsService,
    port: $portService,
    securityHeaders: $headersService,
    robotsTxt: $robotsService,
    sitemap: $sitemapService,
    content: $contentService,
);

$report = $monitor->check(
    host: 'example.com',
    options: new DomainMonitorOptions(
        port: 443,
        timeoutSeconds: 10.0,
        httpMethod: 'GET',
        expectedOrg: 'Example Inc.',
        requiredText: 'Welcome',
    ),
);

echo $report->getStatus()->value;
```

Constructor invariant: `SecurityHeadersService` requires `HttpProbeService` (it needs the HTTP response).

### Normalize input

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HostNormalizer;

$normalizer = new HostNormalizer();
$host = $normalizer->normalizeHost(hostOrUrl: 'https://Example.com:8443/path');
$url = $normalizer->normalizeUrl(url: 'https://Example.com/path');
```

### Probe a URL

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeOptions;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeService;

$result = $probeService->check(
    url: 'https://example.com',
    options: new HttpProbeOptions(
        method: 'GET',
        headers: ['Accept' => 'text/html'],
    ),
);
```

### Check SSL, WHOIS, DNS

```php
$ssl = $sslService->check(host: 'example.com', expectedOrg: 'Example');
$whois = $whoisService->check(host: 'example.com');
$dns = $dnsService->check(host: 'example.com');
```

### Check content, ports, headers, robots, sitemap

```php
$content = $contentService->check(url: 'https://example.com', requiredText: 'Example');
$port = $portService->check(host: 'example.com', port: 443);
$headers = $headersService->check(response: $response);
$robots = $robotsService->check(baseUrl: 'https://example.com');
$sitemap = $sitemapService->check(sitemapUrl: 'https://example.com/sitemap.xml');
```

### Build a report

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainHealthReport;

$report = new DomainHealthReport(
    host: 'example.com',
    probe: $probe,
    ssl: $ssl,
    whois: $whois,
    dns: $dns,
    port: $port,
    content: $content,
    securityHeaders: $headers,
    robotsTxt: $robots,
    sitemap: $sitemap,
);

$status = $report->getStatus(); // ok | warning | critical | unknown
```

### Probe with response reuse

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeWithResponse;

$probeWithResponse = $probeService->probeWithResponse(url: 'https://example.com');
$probeResult = $probeWithResponse->result;
$response = $probeWithResponse->response;

// Reuse response for security headers and content check
$headers = $headersService->check(response: $response);
$content = $contentService->checkFromResponse(response: $response, requiredText: 'Example');
```
